name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Cleveland, Oh
description_short: NACA offers character-based mortgages with no down payment, no closing costs, and no mortgage insurance, prioritizing affordable homeownership over credit scores.
description_long:
The Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America (NACA) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union and has spent 36 years advocating for affordable homeownership and challenging predatory lending practices. The organization has served 3 million people and helped 500,000 homeowners achieve home ownership through character-based lending rather than traditional credit-score-dependent models.

NACA's primary offering is their Best in America Mortgage program, which features no down payment required, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, and interest rates that do not consider credit score in underwriting. As of April 2026, they offer rates as low as 5% for 15-year fixed mortgages, 5.125% for 20-year terms, and 5.625% for 30-year mortgages. Borrowers must attend an "Achieve the Dream Event" (held in multiple cities including Hartford, Little Rock, Milwaukee, Fort Myers, and St. Louis) to become NACA Qualified and access their services. They also provide HUD-approved housing counseling, representing 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally, and have issued 75,000 mortgages with $20 billion in mortgage commitments.

NACA distinguishes itself through its non-profit advocacy model focused on closing the racial wealth disparity gap and its character-based lending philosophy that evaluates borrower circumstances rather than credit history. The organization actively fights predatory landlords, corporate real estate investors, and predatory lenders, positioning itself as both a lender and a social justice organization. Members can access a member portal to upload documents, provide information, and check application status.

The main limitation is that NACA's model requires in-person event attendance and active member participation, which may not be accessible to all borrowers. While their mortgage products are genuinely competitive and designed for underserved populations, the application process is more involved than traditional lenders and is not suited for those seeking online-only, quick-approval mortgage shopping.
pros: No down payment required for qualified borrowers
Zero closing costs or fees included in mortgages
No mortgage insurance requirement regardless of down payment
Credit score not considered in underwriting decisions
Competitive fixed rates (5%-5.625% as of April 2026) below market for qualified borrowers
Provides HUD-approved housing counseling (30% of all HUD counseling nationally)
Character-based lending that considers borrower circumstances beyond credit history
75,000 mortgages issued with $20 billion in mortgage commitments backing their track record
cons: Requires in-person attendance at "Achieve the Dream Events" to become NACA Qualified—not available online
Membership-based model may require participation and activism, which is not appealing to all borrowers
Limited geographic accessibility due to event scheduling (only select cities at specific times)
Less convenient than traditional lenders for quick online application and approval processes
No information provided about underwriting timeline, approval rates, or specific qualification criteria beyond event attendance
best_for: First-time homebuyers with limited savings who cannot afford a down payment
Borrowers with poor or no credit history who are denied by traditional mortgage lenders
Individuals committed to affordable homeownership advocacy and willing to participate in community events
People seeking below-market mortgage rates without penalties for lower credit scores
diagnosis: NACA is best for first-time homebuyers with limited savings or damaged credit who can attend in-person qualifying events and value a mission-driven, advocacy-focused lender. The main caveat is that their application process requires event participation rather than online convenience, making them unsuitable for borrowers seeking traditional quick-approval mortgage shopping.
services: Character-based mortgage lending (no credit score consideration)
No closing costs or fees mortgages
In-person "Achieve the Dream Events" for membership qualification
Member portal for document upload and application status tracking
Below-market mortgage rate lock-in for qualified members
Advocacy and education against predatory landlords and lenders
Partnership coordination with lenders, servicers, real estate agents, and settlement agents
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
